message:
feat(history): add rule-level analytics queries and suggestion audit table

Implement comprehensive rule-level analytics infrastructure to enable
data-driven pack optimization and effectiveness tracking. This supports
the broader goal of identifying noisy rules (high override rates) and
underutilized patterns.

Changes to history/schema.rs:
- Add RuleMetrics and RuleTrend types for per-rule statistics
- Implement get_rule_metrics() for aggregated rule statistics including:
  - Total hit counts across all commands
  - Allowlist override rates (bypass frequency)
  - First/last seen timestamps for trend analysis
  - Unique command variant counts
  - Trend indicators (rising, falling, stable, new)
- Add get_rule_metrics_for_rule() for single-rule lookups
- Create suggestion_audit table for tracking allowlist suggestion outcomes:
  - Captures accepted/modified/rejected decisions
  - Records pattern, risk level, confidence tier
  - Enables retrospective analysis of suggestion quality
- Add appropriate indexes for timestamp, action, and session_id queries

Changes to history/mod.rs:
- Export new RuleMetrics and RuleTrend types in public API

These analytics enable several key use cases:
1. Identifying rules with >50% override rate for potential refinement
2. Tracking rule usage trends to detect emerging command patterns
3. Auditing suggestion acceptance rates to improve the suggestion engine
4. Supporting pack effectiveness dashboards for infrastructure teams

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
